reasoning: All three correctly reject the 'lazy staff'/flu narrative and identify downstream boarding plus induced demand as the binding constraint, but they differ in rigor and calibration. C is strongest: it grounds the mechanism in Little's Law (correctly stated as L=lambda*W) and Meadows' leverage hierarchy, applies a falsifiability/hard-to-vary test to the blame story, and is the only one to attach explicit, well-separated confidence levels (85% structural cause, 65% boarding dominant) plus a cheap disconfirming diagnostic. A is nearly as good with a clean pattern-signature argument, a concrete leverage-ordered fix, and an honest caveat, but it omits formal quantitative tools and explicit probabilities. B reaches the same conclusion and adds a sharp queueing-theory point about nonlinear waits near 85-90% utilization, but it overclaims slightly ('almost certainly'), hedges less rigorously, and reads as a less disciplined draft with weaker structure and no quantified confidence.
